assertionResults.observation: Public issue #28 records PR #38 final head cf4ce450882f110c92d60e01f8580dc851111eb3 and all-green run 29162394115 covering persisted intents, reload, replacement, confirmation, reorg, timeout, and unknown submission in deterministic wallet fixtures. The recorded checks directly support this assertion: A receipt below the required confirmation threshold remains confirming rather than canonical success.

Public issue #28 records PR #38 final head cf4ce450882f110c92d60e01f8580dc851111eb3 and all-green run 29162394115 covering persisted intents, reload, replacement, confirmation, reorg, timeout, and unknown submission in deterministic wallet fixtures. The recorded checks directly support this assertion: Receipt disappearance or block-hash change demotes the intent to orphaned or reconciling and never leaves it as success.

Public issue #28 records PR #38 final head cf4ce450882f110c92d60e01f8580dc851111eb3 and all-green run 29162394115 covering persisted intents, reload, replacement, confirmation, reorg, timeout, and unknown submission in deterministic wallet fixtures. The recorded checks directly support this assertion: Reload preserves the same owner-scoped reviewed intent, nonce, and hash lineage during reorg reconciliation.

Public issue #28 records PR #38 final head cf4ce450882f110c92d60e01f8580dc851111eb3 and all-green run 29162394115 covering persisted intents, reload, replacement, confirmation, reorg, timeout, and unknown submission in deterministic wallet fixtures. The recorded checks directly support this assertion: Success is restored only after a receipt in the canonical chain reaches the required confirmation threshold.

environment.operatingSystem: GitHub-hosted Linux and independently recorded exact-head validation
environment.stack: Feather SDK, web, RPC, indexer, and transaction fixture suites
